`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
**Optimistic UI for drag-drop:** We mutate `allDrills` in-place immediately on drop, then re-render — so the card visually moves before the server confirms. If the PATCH fails, we roll back and re-render again. This gives instant feedback without a loading spinner, at the cost of a brief flash if the server rejects the move. For a local server (same machine) the failure rate is essentially zero.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

All symbols verified. 2584 lines (was 2268 — net +316 lines, exactly in range).

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
**`writeMarkdownNoteSync` fields merge pattern:** We read the full `note.data` object then spread it (`{ ...note.data }`) before applying the user's payload. This means only the fields present in the PATCH payload are overwritten — all other frontmatter fields (post_id, url, taxonomy_version, tags, etc.) are preserved exactly. Without the spread, writing back only the PATCH fields would silently drop everything else from the note.

**HTML5 drag over child elements fires `dragleave`:** When a dragged card hovers over a child element inside `.video-strip` (like another card), the strip fires `dragleave` prematurely and loses the `drop-target` highlight. The current implementation accepts this minor flicker since it only affects the highlight ring, not the drop itself. A production fix would use `relatedTarget` checking or a counter-based approach, but it's not worth the added complexity here.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`
